content.full_text:
Re:
Flrn Porrrrclr Pnacucrs CouurssroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (9t6) 322-0886
January 12,2001
Boyce Hinman
Administrator
Lambda Letters Project
62L2 Silverton Way
Carmichael, CA 95608
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. ,4.-00-281
Dear Mr. Hinman:
QUESTIONS
Is the Lambda Letters Project required to file reports as a $5,000 filer? If so,
which expenses should be counted toward meeting the $5,000 threshold?
Does Regulation 18616(gX3) exempt part or all of the Project's expenses from the
$5,000 filer requirement?
CONCLUSIONS
Without further information, we are unable to determine whether the Lambda
Letters Project qualified as a $5,000 filer in any one calendar quarter of 2000.
However, it does appear that a portion of your activities do not gualiff as
lobbying activities, such as advocating federal legislative or administrative action,
dues expenses for membership in other civil rights groups, bank f€es, and travel
costs associated with bringing the Project's board and staff to project board
meetings.
No' Regulation 18616(gX:) was intended to grant an exception to organizations
whose primary purpose is not lobbying, but which may from time.to time
' 
Gorernment Code sections 8 1000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections
18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regularions.
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on behalf of the Lambda
Letters Project ("the Project") regarding whether the Project is required to file as a $5,000
filer under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").r
1
2.
I
2


FACTS
You are the Administrator for Lambda Letters Project, an organization that
provides information to its members on proposed state and federal legislation. The
Project has been in existence since 1988 and has traditionally been sdffed with
volunteers. No individual receives payment for making direct contact with state elected
or administrative officials.
The Project prepares sample letters which members may sign and send to
legislators, the Governor, and the President. Your organization periodically uses email to
contact members so that they may, in tum, contact elected officials on issues of a
particularly urgent nature. You did not state what percentage of your work is at the
federal level.
Up until December 19,2000, the Project had expended$Z},77| 58 on various
activities. These activities included: mailing a newsletter to members encouraging them
to contact elected officials; dues for membership in other civil rights organizations; bank
fees; space rental at Pride festivals to recruit new members; and travel costs for board
members to attend bsard meetings and recruit new members at the Pride festivals around
the state. In addition, you have hired a part time staff member who receives $1,000 per
month to perform the administrative tasks associated with maintaining databases and
other clerical functions.
AI\IALYSIS
Section 86115(b) requires periodic reports from:
"Any person who directly or indirectly makes payments-to influence
legislative or administrative action of five thousand dollars ($5,000)
or more in value in any calendar quarter ...."
Section 82002 defines administrative action as:
Boyce Hinman
A-00-281
Page Two
advocate a position on a bill in a regularly-scheduled newsletter. Since your
primary purpose is to advocate support or opposition to legislative or
administrative action, the Project's newsletter would not be exempted from
reporting under this regulation.
". . .the proposal, drafting, development, consideration, amendment,
enactment or defeat by any state agency of any rule, regulation or
other action in any rate-making proceeding or any quasl-legislative
proceeding ...."


Boyce Hinman
4-00-281
Page Three
Legislative action is defined in Section 82037 as
". . . the drafting, introduction, consideration, modification,
enactment or defeat of any bill, resolution, amendment,
report, nomination or other matter by the Legislature or by
either house or any committee, subcommittee, joint or
select committee thereof, or by a member or employee of
the Legislature acting in his official capacity. 'Legislative
action' also means the action of the Governor in approving
or vetoing any bill."
The funds the Project expends to encourage and assist its members to contact their
legislators and the Governor quali$ as "payments to influence." (Section 82045(e).)
Therefore, if a Lambda Letters Project newsletter addresses only state legislative or
administrative action, all costs associated with the newsletter's production and
dissemination should be calculated to determine if the $5,000 threshold is met. In
addition, to the extent members of the public are encouraged to contact their legislators
and the Governor on various pieces of legislation by any other means, such as ernail or at
the Pride festivals, all or a portion of those costs may quali$ as "influencing legislative
or adminishative action." How much should be allocated to calculate the $5,00b
threshold would depend upon the degree to which the Project's activities are focused on
state legislative or administrative action.
Payments for membership dues in other civil rights organizations should not be
calculated, nor should banks fees, or travel costs related to board members' attendance at
board meetings. Since costs associated with lobbying legislative or administrative action
at the federal level do not qualifr, the pro-rata costs orproaucing and mailing a
newsletter containing federal issues may be subtracted to determine whether the $5,000
threshold has been met.
Regulation 1 86 16(9)(3) states
"A filer does not have to report any part of the costs of
producing a regularly published newsletter or periodicat
whose circulation is limited to an organization's members,
employees, shareholders, other affiliated individuals and
those who request or purchase the publication. The
exception applies only to those costs regularly incurred in
publishing and distributing the newsletter or periodical. If
additional costs are incured because the newsletter or
periodical is issued on other than its regular schedule,


Boyce Hinman
A-00-281
Page Four
expanded in circulation, or altered in style, size, or format
for the primary pufpose of tnfluencing or attempting to
influence legislative or administrattve action,such -
u6dililnal costs are reportable under subdivision (0(2XC).,,
[Emphasis added.]
While the language in this regulation would first appear to apply to the Lambda
Letters Project's newsletters, the italicized language indicates that thi; exception is
directed to publicationswith a primary p,rrposi other than lobbying; providing for such
an exception for a n9ws.l9tt9r which is primarily published and distributed to Jr"ourug"
readers to influence legislative or administrative action would nullify the purpose of such
a regulation.
In conclusion, to determine if the Project must file as a $5,000 filer, calculate only
those costs associated with directly or indirectly influen cing state legislative or
administrative action. If those costs total $5,000 in any onJcalendaiquarter (January -
March; April - June; July - september; october - December), the prdect wii be
required to file the Form 645 (copy enclosed) for each quarter the threihold is met. If
you feel that the Project met the threshold during any previous calendar quarter, a Form
645 must be filed with the Secretary of State's om"i i, ,oo, as possible.
If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-
s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant
S. Braaten-Moen,
Reforrn Consultant II
